title: Kernel bootstrap for this Codex session. Read and execute the repository-authoritative prompt at: /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md Run id: adhoc:MacBook-Pro-M5-5:kernel-workspace:25971:run:20260404T090520:52378 Bootstrap receipt path: /Users/masayuki/.config/ker...
summary_text: Sequence 1. Fix run context to the exact run id: `RUN_ID='adhoc:MacBook-Pro-M5-5:kernel-workspace:25971:run:20260404T090520:52378'` `export KERNEL_RUN_ID="$RUN_ID"` 2. Verify specialist availability first (no actions yet), then decide healthy specialist lanes: - `bash scripts/lib/kernel-specialist-picker.sh status` - Prefer dynamic best-by-health candidates from `gemini-cli`, `cursor-cli`, `copilot-cli`. 3. Predeclare live evidence and write bootstrap receipt into repo-local state (not `~/.config`) before acknowledgement: - `export KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_ACTIVE_MODELS_CSV='codex,glm,gemini-cli,cursor-cli'` - `export KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_MANIFEST_LANE_COUNT=6` - `export KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_AGENT_LABELS=true` - `export KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_SUBAGENT_LABELS=true` - `KERNEL_RUN_ID="$RUN_ID" bash scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh write 6 'codex,glm,gemini-cli,cursor-cli' normal` 4. Launch 6 parallel lanes immediately with one role per lane and no serial dependency: - Lane A: codex main/orchestrator lane (Kernel synthesis + routing). - Lane B: codex execution lane (implementation). - Lane C: codex simulation lane dedicated to validation (`gpt-5.3-codex-spark` lane if selectable). - Lane D: glm lane via `kglm ...` (GLM execution path). - Lane E: specialist lane via selected specialist wrapper (e.g., `kgemini`). - Lane F: second specialist lane if available and healthy (e.g., `kcursor` or `kcopilot`). 5. Before first acknowledgement, ensure each lane is live and recorded as success: - Force at least one successful codex, one glm, and at least one specialist provider event in runtime ledger (wrappers do this on success). - Keep all six lanes running in separate parallel contexts. 6. Run health/receipt verification: - `KERNEL_RUN_ID="$RUN_ID" bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh status` - Only then emit acknowledgement with: - `Kernel orchestration is active for this session.` - `Bootstrap target: 6+ lanes (minimum 6).` - `Active models: ...` - `Lane manifest:` with 6+ lanes, each including provider, active agent, and `subagent:subagentN` labels. Critical caveats - The runtime health check in this repo reads `.fugue/kernel-state/...`; a receipt only under `~/.config/...` is insufficient for in-repo health validity. - `active_model_count`, `lane_count`, `manifest_lane_count`, `has_agent_labels`, and `has_subagent_labels` are hard gates; all must satisfy thresholds (active model count minimum is 3). - Ledger evidence is mandatory for a healthy bootstrap: `codex_success >= 1`, `glm_success >= 1` (or degraded-allowed rules), and at least one specialist success provider in normal mode. - `kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh write` transitions lifecycle state to `running`; with `running`, a compact artifact and valid tmux session may be required to avoid `compact-artifact-missing*`/`tmux-session-missing*` reasons. - If `glm` fails twice, you must switch to `degraded-allowed` via `codex-kernel-guard glm-fail ...` and continue with two specialist voices; do not claim normal shape then. - If required specialist lanes cannot be activated live, this is not bootstrap-complete under the prompt rules: fail closed rather than shrinking concurrency.
